local_dependency: Node.js proxy and the CreNexus UXP plugin
known_risks: PSD files, source images, exports, commercial fonts, brushes, and Creative Cloud caches must not be committed.
A flat image has no original layer graph; semantic reconstruction is not lossless PSD recovery.
Real production writes require an authorized live UXP session, a managed source hash, a sandbox duplicate, and explicit approval.
Live Photoshop acceptance remains separate from mock and protocol test success.
next_steps: Run the authorized UXP sandbox workflow with PNG, JPEG, PSD, preview readback, and native PSD reopen evidence.
Extend the reviewed public subject dataset without adding client assets or generated artifacts to Git.
Keep smart-object, text, and complex compositing recipes blocked until their typed implementations pass the same gates.
